steps.title: Locate the disagreement
steps.content: Text 1 argues that mandatory voting makes election results better reflect the whole country's preferences by adding voters who otherwise would not participate. Text 2 responds that forced voters may spend less time learning about choices, so their votes may not reflect their actual preferences after all.

steps.title: Avoid unrelated policies
steps.content: A adds population size, which neither text mentions. B invents other kinds of political engagement. D shifts to making voting easier, another topic outside both texts. Singh and Roy would challenge the representativeness claim itself rather than confirming Text 1's conclusion about national preferences. The answer is C.
